text: Sustained attention is not a single monolithic ability but a family of related capacities with distinct neural substrates. Nilli Lavie's "Load Theory," developed through a series of experiments in the 1990s and 2000s, argues that distraction is not inevitable: when a primary task fully occupies perceptual processing resources, distractors are filtered automatically because there is no spare capacity to process them. The implication is counterintuitive — a harder task, one that genuinely saturates your attention, is less vulnerable to interruption than an easy one. Boredom creates attentional vacancy; deep engagement creates its own shield.
question: According to Nilli Lavie's Load Theory, under what condition are distractors filtered automatically?

options.text: When the observer deliberately suppresses bottom-up attention circuits.
When the task involves motor action rather than pure perception.
When norepinephrine levels are elevated by brief physical exercise.
When the primary task fully occupies available perceptual processing resources.
